content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine defects in the propose-new-bead creation and reconciliation paths. Amendment fr-4-c044f649 is the most critical: the title-only fallback reclassification marks mappings as verified without checking description or scope, which can silently suppress genuinely new follow-up work. Amendment fr-4-b6989cd5 identifies a real gap where journaled bead IDs are trusted without verifying the bead still exists, and fr-4-30c03445 correctly flags that error-swallowing during success reconciliation permanently loses accepted amendments with no retry path.

## Votes

- `fr-4-b6989cd5`: Accept
  The code at lines 2970-2975 returns early with a journaled created_bead_id without verifying the bead still exists in .beads. If the bead was removed (via br close, manual cleanup, or external tooling), the caller receives a stale ID and treats the creation as complete, never recreating or repairing it. The journal match (matching_proposed_bead_created_event) checks active_bead_id, proposed_title, finding_summary, and run_id — all metadata fields — but never confirms the created_bead_id is still a live bead. A br show verification before returning is a minimal, low-risk fix that closes a real defensive gap.
- `fr-4-c044f649`: Accept
  This is the most serious of the three findings. The control flow is: (1) lines 2986-3031 try a strong match — title + full description via existing_bead_matches_replayed_proposed_creation, (2) lines 3034-3056 fall through to a weak match using only eligible_existing_proposed_bead_match which checks only id != active_bead_id and status ∈ {Open, InProgress, Deferred}. All beads reaching this fallback already FAILED the description check in loop (1), yet the code reclassifies them as planned-elsewhere with mapped_bead_verified: true. With generic normalized titles (e.g., 'add error handling', 'improve test coverage'), this maps to the wrong bead, suppresses creation of genuinely new follow-up work, and falsely claims the mapping is verified. The remediation of requiring a richer semantic match before setting verified=true is correct and necessary.
- `fr-4-30c03445`: Accept
  Three error paths in reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success silently return Ok(()): (1) line 935-943 payload listing failure drops ALL propose-new-bead amendments, (2) line 968-977 aggregate parse failure drops ALL amendments, (3) line 987-998 missing metadata drops individual amendments. Since this is the sole processing site for propose-new-bead amendments during success reconciliation, these swallowed errors permanently lose accepted findings with no retry mechanism. The first two cases are especially dangerous — a transient filesystem error or a minor schema change in the aggregate payload silently discards every accepted propose-new-bead amendment for the entire run. Bubbling these as ReconciliationError is the correct approach to enable operator retry.
